prerequisite_rows.note: the pre-closeout claim boundary is only meaningful if the broader internal-compute lane has already been red-teamed and found to fail closed
the repo must already distinguish resumable bounded computation from a broader universality claim
the frozen core-Wasm lane must stay separately gated instead of being over-read as the universality claim
post-core proposal families must still be separate named profiles with explicit promotion boundaries
the pre-closeout report only counts broad-compute bridge posture when accepted-outcome-ready and candidate-only lanes remain explicitly distinct
the terminal contract still lacks one declared universal substrate model
the terminal contract still lacks the explicit universal-machine witness encoding
the terminal contract still lacks the dedicated universality witness benchmark suite
the terminal contract still lacks the single green gate over control, memory, continuation, replay, and witness workloads
the terminal contract still lacks the theory/operator/served verdict split

allowed_pre_closeout_statement: Psionic/Tassadar can now say exactly which disclosure-safe broadness and boundary surfaces are real before the terminal universal-substrate contract starts, but it may not yet claim Turing-complete support or a final universality verdict.
current_true_scopes: named authority-and-economic bridge for broad internal-compute profiles
claim_boundary: this pre-closeout audit freezes the last honest claim boundary before the terminal universal-substrate tranche. It names what is already real, what remains gated, and what still must land before the repo can speak in terminal universality language.
summary: Pre-closeout universality audit keeps satisfied_prerequisites=5, missing_terminal_contract_ids=5, claim_status=Suppressed.
